mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 55 percent of months when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the signal, or the unemployment rise fails to predict negative returns in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer spending and real estate investment, confirming broad-based demand growth that supports corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 10-year trend by more than 1 standard deviation, or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction and rising input costs, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when FEDFUNDS is stable or declining, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative during tightening periods with falling INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that forces central banks to maintain tight policy, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 65 percent of cases when both thresholds are met, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing across different inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and consumer spending capacity, boosting short-term market sentiment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with payroll changes with t-stat below 1.5, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent on a quarterly basis indicates a significant demand slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns in more than 70 percent of instances, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which improves corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns do not materialize in more than 55 percent of months with rising industrial production.
